name: Priya Sharma
chief_complaint: I have the worst headache of my life and my vision is blurry, I feel like my head is going to explode
onset_description: Headache started about 5 hours ago and has been getting progressively worse. Noticed blurry vision about 2 hours ago. Took ibuprofen at home without relief. Coworker insisted she come to the ED when she looked 'really unwell' at work.

headache.description: Severe pounding headache all over, worst in the back of my head

visual_changes.description: Everything looks blurry and I'm seeing little spots floating around
aggravating_factors: trying to read or focus on anything

nausea.description: Very nauseous, vomited once in the car on the way here
nausea.character: persistent nausea with one episode of vomiting

chest_tightness.description: Feels like a band around my chest, not really pain but very uncomfortable

shortness_of_breath.description: Feeling a little breathless, not terrible but noticeable

epistaxis.description: Nose started bleeding about an hour ago, hasn't fully stopped

labs.troponin: 0.06 ng/mL (mildly elevated, cardiac strain from severe hypertension)
labs.CBC: WBC 8.8, Hgb 13.5, Plt 255 - within normal limits
labs.urinalysis: Protein 2+, no blood, no WBC - proteinuria suggesting hypertensive nephropathy
labs.ECG: Sinus tachycardia, left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern, no acute ST changes

social_history.alcohol: Rare, maybe a glass of wine at a celebration
social_history.occupation: Accountant at a large firm, currently very stressed with tax season deadlines
social_history.living_situation: Lives with husband and two teenage children
social_history.exercise: Used to do yoga regularly but hasn't had time for months due to work stress
social_history.diet: Mostly home-cooked Indian food, admits to using a lot of salt in cooking and eating salty snacks at work

conditions.father: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, chronic kidney disease, alive at 76
conditions.mother: Hypertension, had a stroke at age 62, alive at 74 with residual weakness

personality: anxious and overwhelmed, a perfectionist who is used to being in control and is very scared right now
communication_style: Articulate and detailed when describing symptoms. Asks a lot of questions because she wants to understand what is happening. May become tearful when discussing stress. Wants to know every lab result and what it means. Will ask 'Is this a stroke? Am I going to die?'
pain_description_style: Very precise with descriptions, uses vivid language ('my head feels like it's in a vise,' 'pounding like a drum'). Rates pain accurately and doesn't minimize or exaggerate.

critical_findings: Blood pressure 224/132 - severely elevated meeting criteria for hypertensive emergency

End-organ damage: visual changes (hypertensive retinopathy risk), headache with vomiting (cerebral edema risk), epistaxis, elevated creatinine (renal involvement), proteinuria, elevated troponin (cardiac strain)
LVH with strain pattern on ECG indicating chronic uncontrolled hypertension
Absence of focal neurological deficits (important to document - rules out acute stroke)
Negative urine drug screen (rules out sympathomimetic cause)
Strong family history of hypertension, stroke, and kidney disease
Elevated work stress and high-sodium diet as contributing factors
History of migraines - must differentiate from hypertensive encephalopathy
recommended_interventions: IV access with continuous blood pressure monitoring (arterial line consideration)
IV antihypertensive: nicardipine or clevidipine drip (goal: reduce MAP by no more than 25% in first hour)
Stat CT head without contrast to rule out hemorrhagic stroke
Fundoscopic exam to assess for papilledema and retinal hemorrhages
Pain management for headache (avoid NSAIDs given renal function)
ICU admission for blood pressure titration
Stress management and dietary counseling prior to discharge
